name: Honey Garlic
subtitle: The ultimate liquid gold immunity booster
blrNote: Use Raw Coorg Honey from Nature's Nectar or any Kodagu farm stall. Many supermarket honeys are pasteurized (heated to 70°C) and won't ferment. Bengaluru's mild 25°C keeps the honey fluid.

IN.where: Any sabziwala - buy the big "Ooty" garlic for fat cloves

ingredients.name: Raw Honey
substitutions: Must be raw and unfiltered - NO substitute

IN.where: Coorg farm shops, Nature's Basket, or organic stores in Indiranagar/Koramangala

equipment.name: pH strips
equipment.notes: For safety check at 2 weeks - should read below 4.6

tldr: Drown garlic in honey. Flip daily. Forget for a month. Eat the best garlic of your life.

steps.title: Peel & Bruise
steps.instruction: Peel the garlic. Lightly crush each clove with the flat of a knife-don't mash it, just break the surface to release juices. This kick-starts fermentation.
tips: The moisture from the garlic is what activates fermentation in the honey
Use fresh, firm garlic - soft or sprouting cloves introduce off-flavours and can harbour mould
steps.checkpoint: Every clove slightly cracked but intact

steps.title: Jar it
steps.instruction: Fill a jar halfway with garlic. Pour honey over until covered, leaving 2 inches of headspace (it will bubble and rise significantly).
tips: The honey WILL foam and expand - don't overfill
Put the jar on a plate to catch any overflow
Warm the honey jar in a bowl of hot water first so it pours easily and coats every clove
steps.checkpoint: Garlic fully submerged in honey with headspace

steps.title: The Daily Flip
steps.instruction: For the first week, turn the jar upside down once a day to ensure all garlic stays coated. The garlic floats initially - flipping prevents dry spots where mould could form.
tips: Set a daily reminder - consistency matters here
After about a week, the garlic sinks as it absorbs honey
Burp the lid slightly when you flip - the CO2 buildup in the first week can pop a sealed jar
steps.checkpoint: Bubbles forming when you flip - fermentation is active

steps.title: Burp & Age
steps.instruction: Open the lid briefly every day to release CO2 for the first month. After that, burp weekly. The longer you wait, the better it gets. At 1 month it's good. At 6 months it's incredible. At 1 year, it's transcendent.
tips: The honey will thin out to an almost syrupy consistency - this is correct
The garlic becomes jelly-like and sweet with zero bite
Drizzle the infused honey over grilled halloumi or into a hot toddy - it's one of the best condiments you'll ever make
steps.checkpoint: Month 1: honey is thin and garlic is soft

images.caption: Honey Garlic
images.alt: Honey Garlic - The ultimate liquid gold immunity booster

culturalContext.story: Used in folk medicine across cultures as a "winter tonic." The garlic loses its harsh bite and becomes sweet and jelly-like, while the honey becomes thin and infused with garlic essence. In Korea, a similar concept appears as "Maneul-jangajji."
culturalContext.historicalNote: Archaeologists have found edible honey in Egyptian tombs over 3,000 years old. Honey's antimicrobial properties mean this ferment straddles the line between preservation and controlled transformation.
culturalContext.significance: One of the only ferments that genuinely gets better after a full year. Most fermentation is about capturing a "peak" moment - this one just keeps climbing.
culturalContext.funFact: The infused honey drizzled over pizza is a restaurant "secret weapon" in Brooklyn and Portland.

thingsToAccountFor.title: Botulism Risk (Low but real)
thingsToAccountFor.description: Honey is low-water-activity but garlic grows in soil. To be safe, test pH after 2 weeks - it should be below 4.6. If the honey is too thick, add 1 tsp apple cider vinegar to kickstart acidification.

thingsToAccountFor.title: Pasteurized honey won't work
thingsToAccountFor.description: Most commercial honeys are heated, which kills the wild yeasts needed for fermentation. The honey must be raw.

dehydratorIntegration.method: Remove aged garlic cloves, slice thin, dehydrate at 45°C for 10–12 hours.
dehydratorIntegration.result: Honey garlic crisps - sweet, savoury, umami-rich chips.
tips: Incredible crumbled over salads or into ramen

variations.name: Honey Garlic Chilli
variations.description: Add 3–4 dried red chillies. The heat infuses into the honey beautifully.

variations.name: Honey Garlic Ginger
variations.description: Add sliced ginger alongside garlic for an immunity triple-threat.

variations.name: Black Pepper Honey Garlic
variations.description: Add whole black peppercorns - the piperine enhances absorption of garlic's allicin.
